perturbation.original_text: 3. In the event that a Transaction is not consummated and upon originator's written request, or otherwise upon Originator's written request, all Evaluation Material supplied by Originator, Target or their respective Representatives (and all copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part thereof, whether prepared by Originator or any of its 2 <PAGE> Representatives or any other person) shall be returned to Originator or, with the Originator's written permission, destroyed and not retained by Participant or its Representatives in any form or for any reason except as required by law or regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. In the event that a Transaction is not consummated and upon originator's written request, or otherwise upon Originator's written request, all Evaluation Material supplied by Originator, Target or their respective Representatives (and all copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part thereof, whether prepared by Originator or any of its 2 <PAGE> Representatives or any other person) shall be returned to Originator or, with the Originator's verbal permission, destroyed and not retained by Participant or its Representatives in any form or for any reason except as required by law or regulations.
perturbation.explanation: The change replaces the requirement for 'written permission' for destruction of Evaluation Material with 'verbal permission'. This contradicts Section 10, which requires amendments or waivers to be in writing. If the Originator verbally permits destruction, it's unclear if this is enforceable given the written amendment requirement, thus creating an ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agreement may not be amended or waived, in whole or in part, except with the written consent of Originator and Participant. It is understood and agreed that no failure or delay by Originator in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver hereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or future exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power or privilege.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 3 to allow for verbal permission for destruction of Evaluation Material. The contradicted text, section 10, states that any amendment or waiver must be in writing. This creates a direct contradiction because verbal permission would not satisfy the written consent requirement, thus creating an ambiguity as stated in the Perturbation Explanation. The 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition is also adhered to.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Participant hereby covenants and agrees that for a period of eighteen months following the date hereof, it shall not, without the prior written consent of Originator (which will not be unreasonably withheld) directly or indirectly: (a) enter into negotiations, provide advice, provide any form of financial assistance (including, without limitation, debt or equity investments) or otherwise encourage, facilitate or consummate any transaction involving Target (whether structured as an investment, a purchase or assets, a purchase of debt or equity interests (including, without limitation, options, warrants or other securities or instruments convertible into or exercisable or exchangeable for debt or equity interests or equity appreciation rights, phantom equity or similar instruments or rights containing equity-like features), a merger, consolidation, combination, recapitalization, refinancing, restructuring, reorganization or otherwise) without the prior express written consent of Originator; or (b) initiate discussions with respect to the prospective employment of, or hire for employment, any employees of Target or any subsidiary thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Participant hereby covenants and agrees that for a period of eighteen months following the date hereof, it shall not, without the prior written consent of Originator (which will not be unreasonably withheld) directly or indirectly: (a) enter into negotiations, provide advice, provide any form of financial assistance (including, without limitation, debt or equity investments) or otherwise encourage, facilitate or consummate any transaction involving Target (whether structured as an investment, a purchase or assets, a purchase of debt or equity interests (including, without limitation, options, warrants or other securities or instruments convertible into or exercisable or exchangeable for debt or equity interests or equity appreciation rights, phantom equity or similar instruments or rights containing equity-like features), a merger, consolidation, combination, recapitalization, refinancing, restructuring, reorganization or otherwise) without the prior express written consent of Originator; or (b) initiate discussions with respect to the prospective employment of, or hire for employment, any employees of Target or any subsidiary thereof. However, section (b) is waived if any employee is terminated by Target or any subsidiary thereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction. Subsection (b) prohibits hiring Target's employees. However, the added clause 'section (b) is waived if any employee is terminated by Target or any subsidiary thereof' creates uncertainty. It doesn't specify *when* the waiver applies. Can Participant immediately hire terminated employees, or is there a waiting period within the 18 months? It contradicts the intention of the non-circumvention clause by opening a loophole.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Participant hereby covenants and agrees that for a period of eighteen months following the date hereof, it shall not, without the prior written consent of Originator (which will not be unreasonably withheld) directly or indirectly: (a) enter into negotiations, provide advice, provide any form of financial assistance (including, without limitation, debt or equity investments) or otherwise encourage, facilitate or consummate any transaction involving Target (whether structured as an investment, a purchase or assets, a purchase of debt or equity interests (including, without limitation, options, warrants or other securities or instruments convertible into or exercisable or exchangeable for debt or equity interests or equity appreciation rights, phantom equity or similar instruments or rights containing equity-like features), a merger, consolidation, combination, recapitalization, refinancing, restructuring, reorganization or otherwise) without the prior express written consent of Originator; or (b) initiate discussions with respect to the prospective employment of, or hire for employment, any employees of Target or any subsidiary thereof. However, section (b) is waived if any employee is terminated by Target or any subsidiary thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 5(b) by adding a waiver if an employee is terminated by Target. While the original text prohibits hiring Target's employees for 18 months, the addition creates an ambiguity regarding when the waiver applies. This ambiguity, as explained, introduces uncertainty about whether the Participant can immediately hire terminated employees or if a waiting period is required within the 18-month period. This directly contradicts the original intention of the non-circumvention clause, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the Ambiguities definition.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Participant acknowledges and affirms that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it and that Originator shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity. In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that Participant has breached this Agreement, then Participant shall be liable for and shall pay to Originator the reasonable legal fees and expenses and court costs incurred by Originator in connection with any such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Participant acknowledges and affirms that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it and that Originator shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, limiting all other remedies available at law or in equity. In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that Participant has breached this Agreement, then Participant shall be liable for and shall pay to Originator the reasonable legal fees and expenses and court costs incurred by Originator in connection with any such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that 'Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, limiting all other remedies available at law or in equity' it creates a direct conflict with the original text which states that these are 'not deemed to be the exclusive remedies'. The contradiction lies in whether other remedies, such as compensatory damages beyond legal fees, are still available if an equitable remedy proves insufficient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Participant acknowledges and affirms that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it and that Originator shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, limiting all other remedies available at law or in equity. In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that Participant has breached this Agreement, then Participant shall be liable for and shall pay to Originator the reasonable legal fees and expenses and court costs incurred by Originator in connection with any such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the equitable remedies are 'deemed to be the exclusive remedies', directly contradicting the original text (and the contradicted text, which is the same in this case) which states they are 'not deemed to be the exclusive remedies'. This is a direct and irreconcilable conflict concerning the availability of other legal remedies and falls under the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' because it creates uncertainty in key obligations. Therefore, a reasonable reader would recognize a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 14. This agreement shall expire upon the earlier of the date the Transaction is consummated or eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. This agreement shall expire upon the later of the date the Transaction is consummated or eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The change in 'earlier' to 'later' introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 14. Now, the agreement will expire on the *later* of the transaction date or 18 months. But sections 5, which discusses non-circumvention, is limited to eighteen months following the date hereof. If the transaction takes longer than 18 months to consummate, the non-circumvention clause will expire before the entire agreement expires creating a potential inconsistency.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Participant hereby covenants and agrees that for a period of eighteen months following the date hereof, it shall not, without the prior written consent of Originator (which will not be unreasonably withheld) directly or indirectly: (a) enter into negotiations, provide advice, provide any form of financial assistance (including, without limitation, debt or equity investments) or otherwise encourage, facilitate or consummate any transaction involving Target (whether structured as an investment, a purchase or assets, a purchase of debt or equity interests (including, without limitation, options, warrants or other securities or instruments convertible into or exercisable or exchangeable for debt or equity interests or equity appreciation rights, phantom equity or similar instruments or rights containing equity-like features), a merger, consolidation, combination, recapitalization, refinancing, restructuring, reorganization or otherwise) without the prior express written consent of Originator; or (b) initiate discussions with respect to the prospective employment of, or hire for employment, any employees of Target or any subsidiary thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement expires on the *earlier* of the transaction consummation date or eighteen months from the agreement date. The changed text states that the agreement expires on the *later* of those two dates. This directly contradicts section 5, which limits non-circumvention to eighteen months following the agreement date. If the transaction takes longer than 18 months, the non-circumvention clause expires before the agreement, creating a contradiction of the agreement's scope and intended protective measures.
